## Highlights

Second minor bump since the [0.4.0 release](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/tag/0.4.0).
0.5.0 polishes the operator experience across the board — a faster,
clearer `lvlab init`, a matured `lvlab smoke`, color control everywhere,
and safer disk handling — plus a couple of breaking changes. Headline
changes:

- **`lvlab init` overhaul.** Downloads run **concurrently** behind a
    compact live progress table instead of one-at-a-time, and `lvlab init`
    now initializes the **built-in default images with no `Lvlab.yml`
    present** — so a fresh host can seed the catalog before you write a
    manifest. (`createvm --init-cloud-images` is deprecated in favour of
    this single path — see Behavior changes.)
- **`lvlab smoke` matured.** The runner introduced in 0.4.0 gained a
    **live per-VM phase table**, **pipe-safe `--json` / `--yaml`** (stdout
    carries only the machine-readable result; the plan/diagnostics go to
    stderr, so `| jq` and `> out.json` work), a **GiB / ≤80-column plan
    table** with colorized preflight, a **memory-heavy confirmation
    prompt** (`--yes` / `-y` to bypass for CI), and a **bounded teardown**
    so a slow-to-poweroff guest (Ubuntu without a guest agent) no longer
    stalls the run. Also fixed: DHCP leases are resolved **by domain**
    rather than a self-pinned MAC, so concurrent batches verify reliably.
- **Color control, consistent everywhere.** A global **`--no-color`**
    flag (and the `NO_COLOR` env var) disables styled output across
    `lvlab`, `createvm`, and `deletevm` — genuinely plain text, not just
    "color off but still bold". **Global flags are accepted in either
    position**: `lvlab --no-color smoke` and `lvlab smoke --no-color` are
    equivalent (same for `-v` / `-q`), the cobra/kubectl "flags anywhere"
    feel.
- **One-time console password + SSH hint on `lvlab up`.** First boot now
    surfaces a generated console password (shown once, not retrievable)
    and a ready `ssh user@ip` hint — the same UX `createvm` already had,
    now shared.
- **Tabular `lvlab status`.** Machines and images render as Rich tables;
    the images table merges the built-in catalog with the manifest and
    labels each image's source.
- **Safer image downloads.** A clean error on download failure plus
    gzip / HTTP-416 resume fixes, so a flaky mirror or an interrupted
    transfer recovers instead of corrupting the cache.

## Breaking changes

- **The `lvlab capabilities` subcommand was removed.** It was unused;
    drop any scripts or aliases that call it.

## Behavior changes

- **`lvlab up` disks now default to standalone copies, not backing-file
    overlays.** Every `up` disk used to be a qcow2 overlay on the shared
    `cloud-images/` cache, so pruning a cached image could silently break
    a running VM. The default is now `copy` (a self-contained `cp` +
    best-effort `qemu-img resize`), which makes `lvlab images clean` safe
    by construction and matches what `createvm` already does. The trade is
    disk space: each VM now takes the full image size. **Opt back into the
    old space-efficient behavior** with `config_defaults.disk_strategy: backing` (or per-disk `disks[*].strategy: backing`) — backing mode
    loses no capability and logs a loud "don't clean the cache while this
    VM exists" warning. Existing disks are untouched; only newly created
    disks follow the new default.
- **`createvm --init-cloud-images` is deprecated.** It still works, but
    prefer `lvlab init` — the single image-init path, which also seeds the
    built-in defaults with no manifest.
- **`createvm --ip4 dhcp` / `default` / `auto` now means DHCP.** These
    sentinels (and omitting `--ip4`) request a DHCP guest instead of being
    mangled into an invalid static address; an actually-invalid `--ip4`
    now fails with a clear message.

## Install / upgrade

```bash
uv tool install --upgrade \
  https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/download/0.5.0/tkc_lvlab-0.5.0-py3-none-any.whl
```

The same wheel installs three console scripts: `lvlab` (manifest
workflow), `createvm`, and `deletevm` (one-off VMs). Host package list
per distro is in
[`scripts/host-bootstrap.sh`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.5.0/scripts/host-bootstrap.sh).

## Supported hosts

The supported host matrix is unchanged from 0.4.0:

- **Debian 12** bookworm (Python 3.11)
- **Debian 13** trixie (Python 3.13)
- **AlmaLinux 10** (Python 3.12)
- **Fedora 44** (Python 3.14)

That's the host matrix (what you run `lvlab` *on*) — distinct from the
8-image **guest** catalog (`debian11/12/13`, `ubuntu2204/2404`,
`almalinux9/10`, `fedora44`). See
[`docs-extra/host-validation.md`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.5.0/docs-extra/host-validation.md)
for the procedure and the current matrix.

## Internal / toolchain

Not user-facing, but worth knowing if you read or build the code:

- **Shared output helper (`utils/output.py`).** One Rich table style,
    TTY-vs-pipe detection, a color-gated `get_console`, and a
    `NO_COLOR`-aware `secho` wrapper — the foundation the `status` / `init`
    / `smoke` displays converge on.
- **`GlobalFlagGroup`** (a `typer.core.TyperGroup` subclass) hoists the
    root global flags so they parse in any position; a globals-only command
    line (`lvlab --no-color`) shows the full help rather than erroring.
- **Snapshot teardown** uses a one-shot `undefine --snapshots-metadata`
    across both `deletevm` and `lvlab destroy`.
